topic: Configure storage account encryption and customer-managed keys
key_facts:
The managed identity that is associated with the storage account must have these permissions at a minimum to access a customer-managed key in Azure Key Vault:

 
 wrapkey 

 unwrapkey 

 get 

 
 For more information about key permissions, see Key types, algorithms, and operations .
Azure storage encryption supports RSA and RSA-HSM keys of sizes 2048, 3072 and 4096.
When you specify a customer-managed key, that key is used to protect and control access to the key that encrypts your data.

Note

 Azure Key Vault and Azure Key Vault Managed HSM support the same APIs and management interfaces for configuration of customer-managed keys.
Azure Storage uses the managed identity to which the Azure Key Vault admin granted permissions in step 1 to authenticate access to Azure Key Vault via Microsoft Entra ID.
There is no additional action required on your part to ensure that your data remains encrypted.

Automatically update the key version

 To automatically update a customer-managed key when a new version is available, omit the key version when you enable encryption with customer-managed keys for the storage account.
